g.data-narration: Welcome to Introduction to Trees - a fundamental hierarchical data structure in computer science.
A tree is a non-linear data structure with nodes connected by edges, organizing data hierarchically from a root.
g.text: A tree is a non-linear, hierarchical data structure consisting of
nodes connected by edges. It organizes data in a parent-child relationship
that branches outward from a single starting node called the root.

g.data-narration: Here's a visual example: Node A is the root, B/C/D are its children, E/F/G/H/I are descendants, and E/H/I/C/G are leaves.
Key tree terms: Root is the topmost node with no parent. Parent has children. Child is connected below. Siblings share the same parent.
Edge connects parent to child. Path is a sequence of nodes and edges. Ancestors are above a node, descendants are below it.

g.text: A → B → F is a path

g.data-narration: Degree of a node = number of children. Degree of tree = maximum degree among all nodes. In our tree, degree of tree = 3 (node A has 3 children).
g.text: Degree of Node vs Degree of Tree
Degree of Node = number of children
Degree of Tree = maximum degree of any node = 3

g.data-narration: Level starts at 0 for root. Depth = edges from root. Height = longest path from node to leaf. Tree height = height of root = 3.

g.text: • Depth = edges from root to node
• Height = edges on longest path to leaf

g.data-narration: A forest is a collection of disjoint trees. Removing the root from a tree creates a forest of subtrees.

g.text: Three disjoint trees rooted at B, C, D

g.data-narration: Important properties: n nodes = n-1 edges. Max nodes at level i = d^i. Max nodes in height h = 2^(h+1) - 1.

g.text: Property 2: Max Nodes at Level i

g.data-narration: Binary tree: max 2 children, left/right are distinct. General tree: any number of children. Binary ≠ degree 2 tree!

g.text: Any number of children per node

g.data-narration: Array representation: nodes stored by level-order index. Left child = 2i+1, Right child = 2i+2. Parent = floor((i-1)/2).
Linked representation uses structs with data, left pointer, and right pointer. Most common for binary trees.
Left child-right sibling converts any general tree to binary tree form. leftChild→first child, rightSibling→next sibling.

g.text: Converts general tree to binary tree using 2 pointers:
